resources.string: A focused sample that shows lifecycle and health state, triggers monitored main-thread work, and renders the latest parsed Falcon event without touching legacy internal APIs.
Use the built-in controls to generate a slow runnable or ANR on the main thread, then inspect the event payload rendered below.
Crosses the sample slow-runnable threshold without forcing a full ANR, which makes it the safe first step for validation.
Locks the main thread long enough to trigger the ANR path end-to-end. Use it only when you are ready to inspect a real capture.
Enter any duration to test threshold behavior, for example 250ms for “no event”, 800ms for a slow runnable, or 4000ms for ANR.
This panel is driven by Falcon callbacks and `FalconDumpPayloadEventAdapter`, not by local placeholder data.
Trigger a slow runnable or ANR to inspect the latest captured message and payload summary.
No dumper payload has been captured yet.
The timeline only records Falcon callback output, so each line reflects a real capture rather than a button click echo.
